Data Protection and Retention Workflow Note for Chanthara Renewables Public Company Limited, a BGX-listed issuer operating under PDPA and Siam securities rules.
Scope: we process personal data (as defined under the PDPA) of employees, directors, candidates, vendors, and investors for financing, ESG/climate reporting, regulatory disclosure to the Siam Securities Commission, and cross-border contracting.
Purposes include payroll and benefits, supplier due diligence, whistleblowing management, cyber incident response, drafting offering materials, and evaluating a pilot tokenization of renewable credits and receivables.
MNPI handling: non-public information (e.g., unannounced sustainability-linked financing, draft crypto wallet integration for employee benefit tokens) is subject to information barriers, insider lists, and blackout windows prior to BGX announcements.
Example: a confidential term sheet with Nava River Bank (Vientiane) for a sustainability-linked revolving credit facility (pricing grid TSRR 3M + 210 bps, margin step-downs tied to Scope 2 emissions) is MNPI and shared under NDA on a need-to-know basis.
Example: a draft MOU with Mekhala Digital Asset Exchange to tokenize carbon offsets is maintained in a restricted drive; no selective disclosure to analysts, vendors, or contractors is permitted.
Example: we are investigating a suspected ransomware attempt affecting a non-production OT monitoring node; incident details and remediation timelines remain confidential until disclosed via SSC-compliant channels.
Retention: insider list entries (names, roles, timestamps) are retained for 5 years after the relevant BGX disclosure; cybersecurity logs for 365 days; recruitment records for 1 year unless longer retention is consented or required by law.
Transfers: personal data may be transferred to processors SoraCloud SG Pte. Ltd. and Hinoki Data KK for hosting and backup; PDPA Section 28 safeguards, intercompany rules, and standard contractual clauses approved by the Siam Data Board apply.
DSAR: data subjects may submit access, correction, deletion, objection, and portability requests to the DPO, Khun Ms. Arisa Boonsong, at privacy@chanthara-renewables.co.th; we respond within 30 days subject to lawful exemptions.
Insider list protocol: during the Q3 green bond consideration and tokenization pilot, wall-crossed functions are recorded by Ms. Boonsong; all such persons are in trading blackout until the final BGX filing is published.
Commercial contacts, auditors, and rating agencies may receive anonymized ESG metrics and high-level cyber risk narratives, but no deal terms, wallet addresses, draft pricing triggers, or remediation dates may be shared pre-announcement.
Educational/marketing: our public webinar “Crypto wallets and climate KPIs 101” explains vocabulary like wallets, insider lists, and blackout windows for general awareness only and contains no market-sensitive information.
Operations-only: in the HR app, “wallet” means an internal allowance balance for staff meals and has no relation to digital asset custody or trading.
Tipping prohibition: employees must not tip suppliers or consultants about unannounced cyber remediation, carbon credit purchases, or expected deal timing; breaches may be reported to compliance@chanthara-renewables.co.th.
Where the lawful basis is consent, we record consent logs with IP and timestamp and allow withdrawal without detriment, except where retention is required to establish, exercise, or defend legal claims under SSC and BGX rules.
